title: SAFETY comments on unsafe impl Send are exemplary
description: Both Device (wolftpm/src/device.rs) and WolfTpmLinuxDev / WolfTpmSwtpm (wolftpm-tss/src/connection.rs) have thorough SAFETY comments on unsafe impl Send, citing: (1) no process-global mutable state in wolfTPM, with specific files verified (src/tpm2.c, tpm2_wrap.c); (2) stable heap address via Box; (3) non-Sync so concurrent shared references are prevented; (4) exact wolfTPM version pinned with a forward-maintenance note. A future contributor should not 'simplify' these to bare unsafe impl Send; the reasoning is load-bearing. If wolfTPM upgrades past fbbf6fe, the SAFETY note explicitly flags a re-audit requirement.
